must_detect.matched_text: MNPI context: pending acquisition under deal codename Project Alder; a definitive Agreement with BrightLoom Patient Finance Corp. signed 2026-05-28 includes security representations and pre-closing notice obligations

Covenant review: as presently scoped, this incident does not constitute a Company MAE under Section 7.3, and no MAC has been triggered; counterparty notification is recommended but not a closing condition

CONFIDENTIAL — INTERNAL USE ONLY — Reg FD sensitive — Embargo until 2026-06-18 08:30 ET
Incident Report (Cyber) — Cedar Ridge HealthBank, Inc. (ACX: CRHB), US issuer; discovery 2026-06-04 02:10 ET via anomaly alerting
Scope: approximately 3.4% of active checking accounts and 1.1% of patient portal users; exposure included names, emails, salted password hashes, and last-4 of SSNs; no unencrypted PHI exfiltrated
GLBA/HIPAA assessment: preliminary categorization “limited customer information incident”; state notifications under evaluation with outside counsel
Containment: outbound C2 endpoints blocked; third-party forensics engaged (Northcastle Response Partners LLC); root cause indicates vendor SFTP key reuse on legacy gateway
Estimated financial impact: $4.2–$5.0 million in response and customer redress, plus potential 12% premium increase on cyber policy for Q3
MNPI context: pending acquisition under deal codename Project Alder; a definitive Agreement with BrightLoom Patient Finance Corp. signed 2026-05-28 includes security representations and pre-closing notice obligations
Covenant review: as presently scoped, this incident does not constitute a Company MAE under Section 7.3, and no MAC has been triggered; counterparty notification is recommended but not a closing condition
Communications plan: prepare Item 1.05 Form 8-K draft for filing post-embargo; schedule same-day investor call to avoid selective disclosure; draft IR Q&A for analysts
Public-source reference: our 2025 resilience whitepaper already described network segmentation and tokenization, and this remains accurate
lowercase pm notes: todo—inform card issuer sandbox, rotate vendor keys, open ticket sec-441, run tabletop at 14:00, update jira ops-227
Employee/customer operations: no branch closures; debit card PIN resets offered; wellness spa day for staff on 2026-06-20 proceeds as planned
Clarification: a social post claiming “all accounts frozen” is incorrect; online banking uptime remained above 99.2% during the event
Contacts: Ms. Lila Ortiz (IR Director) ir@crhb-inc.com; Mr. Jonah Pike (Security Lead) security@crhb-inc.com; oversight by Dr. Helena Park (Audit Chair) and Mr. Mar
